dimensions.name: API Security
dimensions.description: Evaluates basic security practices for Flask APIs. CORS must be configured to restrict allowed origins (flask-cors with origins list, not wildcard in production). All user-supplied input passed to the LLM must be validated and sanitized server-side — never trust the client. Basic rate limiting should be implemented (flask-limiter or similar) to prevent API abuse. Sensitive routes should check for an API key or auth header. The Flask debug mode must be disabled in production (debug=False or use an env variable).

dimensions.name: Frontend LLM Integration
dimensions.description: Evaluates how the JavaScript frontend handles LLM API calls via the Flask backend. Fetch/axios calls should be async with proper await/then chains. Loading states (spinners, disabled buttons) must be shown while the API call is in progress to prevent double-submission. Error states should be surfaced to the user with a readable message (not just console.log). Streaming responses can use EventSource (SSE) for incremental display. The API base URL should come from a config constant, not be hardcoded per-call.
